content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding on dict mutations

fields.summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding on dict mutations

content:
type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Yes, keep_features_dicts_synced decorates update() too (recomputes _column_requires_decoding from self.items() after the call completes) - implement your deep-merge logic inside update()'s body freely, sync is automatic and doesn't care about internal logic. My update() now takes *args, **kwargs and calls super().update(*args, **kwargs); feel free to change the body as long as you keep the @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on it.

fields.summary: Yes, keep_features_dicts_synced decorates update() too (recomputes _column_requires_decoding from self.items() after the call completes) - implement your deep-merge logic inside update()'s body freely, sync is automatic and doesn't care about internal logic. My update() now takes *args, **kwargs and calls super().update(*args, **kwargs); feel free to change the body as long as you keep the @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on it.

content:
type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Done: added keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + decorated __setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear/__or__/__ior__ on Features; update() now forwards *args/**kwargs to dict.update so your deep-merge logic can live inside it freely, decorator recomputes _column_requires_decoding after any call completes.
